query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "a survey of large audio language models: generalization, trustworthine" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) by addressing the "Trustworthiness" aspect of the user's survey topic through the specific mechanism of evaluating reasoning quality, factuality, and transparency (Chain-of-Thought) in Large Audio Language Models (LALMs). It provides essential empirical baseline data and methodological frameworks for the "Trustworthiness" dimension of the user's specific domain (Audio LLMs), which is a canonical component of any comprehensive survey on this subject.
